title: 8 Herbal Creams For Bladder Infection
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful treatment for bladder infections. They work by reducing inflammation and fighting off the bacteria that cause the infection. However, herbal teas can also relieve bladder infections in a different way. When you drink herbal tea, the active ingredients are absorbed into your body and can help to calm your bladder and reduce any discomfort.  One of the main reasons herbal teas can be helpful is that they contain anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help to reduce the swelling and irritation in your bladder, making it less painful and uncomfortable. For example, Equisetum arvense, also known as horsetail, is a herb that is commonly used to treat urinary tract infections. It contains silica, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.  Another herb that can be helpful is Echinacea purpurea. This herb is known for its ability to boost the immune system and fight off infections. It can help to reduce the severity of symptoms and promote healing in the bladder. Some people also find that Echinacea helps to prevent future infections.  Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, is another herb that can be helpful in treating bladder infections. It contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe and calm the bladder. This can make it less painful to urinate and reduce any discomfort or burning.  Drinking herbal teas can be a safe and natural way to relieve bladder infections. They can be a helpful addition to other treatments, such as antibiotics, and can be used in combination with other natural remedies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense creams contains silicic acid and equisetic acid, which are active constituents that help alleviate bladder infections. Silicic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and irritation in the urinary tract. Equisetic acid has antimicrobial properties, killing bacteria that cause infections. The creams also contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties, helping to protect the bladder lining from damage. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Equisetum arvense creams can help to alleviate symptoms of bladder infections, promoting healing and relief.
remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal creams for bladder infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Equisetum arvense roots. Wash them with water.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried Equisetum arvense roots into a fine powder.
Mix 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Equisetum arvense powder in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat (100°F) until the beeswax melts. Let it cool, then store in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains several bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, glycoproteins, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce bladder inflammation and kill bacteria that cause infections. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams have been shown to increase the production of white blood cells, which fight off infections in the bladder. The glycoproteins in Echinacea purpurea creams have been found to stimulate the production of cytokines, which help to reduce inflammation in the bladder. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Echinacea purpurea creams can help alleviate symptoms of bladder infections and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for bladder infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, and 10 drops of tea tree oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted oil and beeswax. Let it steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add the shea butter, olive oil, and tea tree oil to the strained mixture. Stir well until everything is fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the creams in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, and flavonoids like licoricidin and licorisoflavan A. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and protect the urinary tract. Glycyrrhizin also has a mild diuretic effect, which can help increase urine production and flush out bacteria from the bladder. The antiseptic properties of flavonoids further aid in preventing the growth of harmful bacteria that cause bladder infections. By reducing inflammation and fighting off bacteria, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help alleviate symptoms of bladder infections and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for bladder infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Combine 1 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder in a saucepan. Heat on low until the powder dissolves.
Remove the saucepan from heat and add 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil. Stir until the beeswax melts.
Let the mixture cool and thicken. This may take about 30 minutes to an hour.
Once the mixture has thickened, pour it into a container and let it cool completely. Your glycyrrhiza glabra cream is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams contains bioactive constituents like astragaloside IV, isoflavones, and polysaccharides that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the bladder and prevent bacterial growth, which can contribute to bladder infections. Astragaloside IV has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can exacerbate bladder infection symptoms. The polysaccharides in Astragalus membranaceus creams also have immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune system's response to the infection. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Astragalus membranaceus creams may help alleviate bladder infection symptoms and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal creams for bladder infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of astragalus membranaceus root powder, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Boil the distilled water and let it cool. Mix astragalus membranaceus root powder with the cooled water to create a paste.
Melt the coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl.
Add the astragalus membranaceus paste to the melted mixture and stir well. Continue heating until the mixture is smooth and combined.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool. Pour into a container and let it solidify. Once solid, your astragalus membranaceus cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata creams contains aucubin and baicalein, which are active constituents that help to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause bladder infections. Aucubin has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the bladder and reduce irritation, while baicalein has antimicrobial properties that kill off the bacteria that cause the infection. The creams also contain other compounds like sitosterol and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and fight off infection. Plantago lanceolata creams have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria. By reducing inflammation and killing off bacteria, Plantago lanceolata creams can help to alleviate symptoms of bladder infections and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Plantago lanceolata herbal creams for bladder infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Plantago lanceolata leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Dry the Plantago lanceolata leaves in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours or until they are crumbly.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Combine the ground leaves with the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler. Heat the mixture until the beeswax melts.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a container. Store the cream in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Berberis vulgaris creams contains berberine, a bioactive compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Berberine helps to fight bacteria that cause bladder infections, such as E. coli, by inhibiting their growth and reproduction. It also reduces inflammation and soothes the bladder, providing relief from symptoms like pain and burning during urination. The alkaloid berberine also has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the bladder from damage caused by free radicals. The combination of antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties of berberine in Berberis vulgaris creams makes it a potential natural remedy for bladder infections.
remedies.image_alt: Berberis vulgaris herbal creams for bladder infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Berberis vulgaris root powder, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of essential oil (optional).
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the Berberis vulgaris root powder to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Add vitamin E oil and essential oil (if using). Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool completely before use. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale creams contains compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and carotenoids. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections in the bladder. The saponins in Taraxacum officinale creams have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that cause urinary tract infections. The flavonoids and carotenoids in the cream have antioxidant properties, which help protect the bladder from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Taraxacum officinale creams may provide relief from symptoms of bladder infections, such as pain and urgency.
remedies.image_alt: Taraxacum officinale herbal creams for bladder infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Taraxacum officinale flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Combine the flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan and heat on low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, pressing on the solids to get as much liquid as possible.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax on low heat. Add the liquid from the cheesecloth and stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once set, your Taraxacum officinale cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are active constituents that give it its healing properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause bladder infections. The flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate the infection-causing bacteria. The anti-inflammatory properties of triterpenoids can also help soothe the bladder and reduce pain and discomfort associated with bladder infections. By applying Calendula officinalis creams to the affected area, it can help promote healing and reduce the risk of further infection.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for bladder infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Combine the dried Calendula flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan and heat over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Add the shea butter to the liquid mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated and the mixture has cooled.
Pour the mixture into a container and store it in the fridge. Use 1-2 teaspoons as needed to relieve bladder infection symptoms.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent bladder infections from forming. Some herbs like uva ursi and juniper berries have antiseptic properties that help fight off bacteria that can cause infections. These herbs can help keep the urinary tract clean and reduce the risk of bladder infections.
faq_safe: Herbal teas like uva ursi and juniper berry may help with bladder infections, but consuming them daily is not recommended. Over time, these herbs can weaken the bladder's natural ability to function. It's best to drink them in moderation, so their benefits can be enjoyed without harming your bladder.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with bladder infections, but it takes time to see results. Some people notice relief within 2-3 days, while others may take a week or more to feel better. It's essential to drink the tea regularly and be patient, as the body needs time to heal and recover.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for a bladder infection can be most effective in the evening. This is because many herbal teas, such as uva ursi and juniper, work best when you're not urinating, allowing their healing properties to stay in your system longer.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for bladder infections. Some herbs, like saw palmetto and juniper berries, can increase the risk of bleeding when taken with blood thinners. Others, like uva ursi, may affect blood sugar levels. It's best to check the labels and know what you're putting in your tea to avoid any potential problems.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbal teas, like juniper and saw palmetto, may affect the uterus and cause problems. However, others like peppermint and ginger are generally considered safe in small amounts. It's essential to research the specific tea you're considering and its potential effects on pregnancy.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's generally safe to try herbal teas for bladder infections. However, some herbs can pass into breast milk and affect your baby. For example, peppermint and dandelion teas are often used, but ginger and sage teas might be better choices. Always read labels and follow instructions carefully to minimize risks.
